ucs.title: [LLM] Whitefly Vcrodat loader: signed AV/security-vendor binary side-loading malicious DLL from user-writable directory
ucs.description: Hunts Whitefly's signature TTP — relocating a legitimately-signed third-party security-vendor executable (Sophos, F-Secure, Symantec, Sysinternals, Trend Micro, etc.) into a user-writable path so that a malicious sibling DLL is side-loaded via search-order hijacking. This is how the Vcrodat backdoor (a.k.a. Trojan.Vcrodat / Backdoor.Vcrodat) was implanted during the SingHealth and other Singapore-targeted intrusions and is a unique combination of T1574.001 + T1036.005 + T1027.013 that very few crews use this consistently.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as command_line values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.process_hash) as loader_hash values(Processes.process_company) as loader_company values(Processes.original_file_name) as loader_original_name from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_company IN ("Sophos*","F-Secure*","Symantec*","Trend Micro*","Sysinternals*","Kaspersky*","McAfee*","ESET*") OR Processes.process_name IN ("rstrui.exe","ssr.exe","cleanwipe.exe","fsma32.exe","fsav32.exe","fsorsp.exe","procexp.exe","procexp64.exe","sgshell.exe")) (Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\PerfLogs\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Windows\\Temp\\*") NOT Processes.process_path="*\\Program Files*" NOT Processes.process_path="*\\Windows\\System32*" NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by host Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process_id
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| join type=inner host process_id [ | tstats summariesonly=true values(Filesystem.file_path) as sibling_dll_paths values(Filesystem.file_name) as sibling_dll_names from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" NOT Filesystem.file_path="*\\Windows\\*" NOT Filesystem.file_path="*\\Program Files*" by host Filesystem.process_id | rename Filesystem.process_id as process_id ]
| where mvcount(sibling_dll_paths) > 0
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// Whitefly / Vcrodat — vendor-signed loader in a user-writable directory side-loads a sibling DLL
let _suspect_dirs = dynamic([@"\Users\Public\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\Users\"]);
let _vendor_companies = dynamic(["Sophos","F-Secure","F Secure","Symantec","Trend Micro","Sysinternals","Kaspersky","McAfee","ESET","Bitdefender"]);
let _vendor_loader_names = dynamic(["rstrui.exe","ssr.exe","cleanwipe.exe","fsma32.exe","fsav32.exe","fsmb32.exe","fsorsp.exe","procexp.exe","procexp64.exe","sgshell.exe"]);
let SuspiciousLoaders = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where (ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has_any (_vendor_companies))
         or (FileName in~ (_vendor_loader_names))
    | where FolderPath has_any (_suspect_dirs)
    | where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files")
    | where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\System32")
    | project LoaderTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              InitiatingProcessId = ProcessId,
              LoaderName = FileName,
              LoaderPath = FolderPath,
              LoaderSHA256 = SHA256,
              LoaderCompany = ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName,
              LoaderOriginalName = ProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName,
              LoaderCmd = ProcessCommandLine;
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName endswith ".dll"
| where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\")
| where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files")
| join kind=inner SuspiciousLoaders on DeviceId, InitiatingProcessId
| where Timestamp between (LoaderTime .. LoaderTime + 5m)
| where FolderPath =~ LoaderPath              // sibling-of-loader = classic search-order hijack
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          LoaderName, LoaderCompany, LoaderOriginalName, LoaderPath, LoaderSHA256, LoaderCmd,
          SideLoadedDll = FileName, SideLoadedDllPath = FolderPath, DllSHA256 = SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Whitefly / Vcrodat — sysmon-based detection. Requires Sysmon EID 1 (process create) + EID 7 (image load).
let _suspect_dirs = dynamic([@"\Users\Public\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\Users\"]);
let _vendor_signers = dynamic(["Sophos","F-Secure","F Secure","Symantec","Trend Micro","Sysinternals","Kaspersky","McAfee","ESET","Bitdefender"]);
let SuspiciousLoaders = WindowsEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 1
    | extend Image = tostring(EventData.Image),
             OriginalFileName = tostring(EventData.OriginalFileName),
             Signature = tostring(EventData.Signature),
             ProcessGuid = tostring(EventData.ProcessGuid),
             User = tostring(EventData.User),
             CommandLine = tostring(EventData.CommandLine)
    | where Signature has_any (_vendor_signers)
    | where Image has_any (_suspect_dirs)
    | where not(Image startswith @"C:\Program Files")
    | where not(User endswith "$")
    | project LoaderTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Image, OriginalFileName, Signature, ProcessGuid, User, CommandLine;
WindowsEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 7
| extend ImageLoaded = tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded),
         ChildImage = tostring(EventData.Image),
         ProcessGuid = tostring(EventData.ProcessGuid),
         LoadedSignature = tostring(EventData.Signature)
| where ImageLoaded endswith ".dll"
| where not(ImageLoaded startswith @"C:\Windows\")
| where not(ImageLoaded startswith @"C:\Program Files")
| where ImageLoaded has_any (_suspect_dirs)
| join kind=inner SuspiciousLoaders on Computer, ProcessGuid
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, User, LoaderImage = Image, OriginalFileName, LoaderSignature = Signature, ImageLoaded, LoadedSignature, CommandLine
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Whitefly Vcrodat Loader - Vendor-Signed Binary Side-Loading DLL From User-Writable Path
id: 8a7f5d2c-9e4b-47a6-bf18-7c3a1e5f9a02
status: experimental
description: Detects Whitefly (G0107) Vcrodat-style DLL search-order hijack where a legitimately signed security-vendor executable is relocated to a user-writable directory so a sibling malicious DLL is loaded.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0107/
  - https://symantec-enterprise-blogs.security.com/blogs/threat-intelligence/whitefly-espionage-singapore
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.privilege_escalation
  - attack.t1574.001
  - attack.t1036.005
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_company:
    Company|contains:
      - 'Sophos'
      - 'F-Secure'
      - 'Symantec'
      - 'Trend Micro'
      - 'Sysinternals'
      - 'Kaspersky'
      - 'McAfee'
      - 'ESET'
      - 'Bitdefender'
  selection_path:
    Image|contains:
      - '\Users\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\AppData\'
      - '\Public\'
      - '\PerfLogs\'
      - '\Windows\Temp\'
  filter_legit:
    Image|startswith:
      - 'C:\Program Files\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\'
      - 'C:\Windows\System32\'
      - 'C:\Windows\SysWOW64\'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_company and selection_path and not filter_legit and not filter_machine_acct
fields:
  - Image
  - OriginalFileName
  - Company
  - CommandLine
  - User
  - ParentImage
falsepositives:
  - Portable copies of legitimate AV/security tools run from removable media or jump-bag directories during incident response
  - Software-update staging that briefly drops vendor binaries into ProgramData
level: high
ucs.rationale: Symantec's Whitefly write-up (March 2019) and the SingHealth COI report both identify Vcrodat's distribution mechanism as the dropping of a legitimately-signed third-party security-software executable into a non-default directory together with a malicious DLL bearing the name the loader expects on its search path. Whitefly was repeatedly observed using Sophos, F-Secure and Symantec utilities for t
